_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Symptoms due to impaired left ventricular myocardial function, presenting with dyspnea and fatigue limiting exercise tolerance, fluid retention characterized by pulmonary and peripheral edema.
Symptoms of heart failure including dyspnea, orthopnea, edema, pain from hepatic congestion, abdominal distention from ascites, fatigue, and weakness.

Acute and subacute presentations (days to weeks) with shortness of breath at rest and/or with exertion, orthopnea, paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea, and right upper quadrant discomfort due to acute hepatic congestion (right heart failure).
Palpitations, with or without lightheadedness (if atrial or ventricular tachyarrhythmias develop).
Chronic presentations (months) with fatigue, anorexia, abdominal distension, and peripheral edema.
Pulsus alternans phenomenon characterized by evenly spaced alternating strong and weak peripheral pulses.
Apical impulse: laterally displaced past the midclavicular line (indicative of left ventricular enlargement).
S3 gallop: a low-frequency, brief vibration occurring in early diastole.
Electrocardiogram (ECG) findings of acute or prior myocardial infarction, acute ischemia, or rhythm abnormalities (e.g., atrial fibrillation).
Chest X-ray findings including cardiac-to-thoracic width ratio above 50%, cephalization of the pulmonary vessels, Kerley B-lines, and pleural effusions.
Elevated Cardiac troponin (T or I), BNP (or NT-proBNP) levels, abnormal complete blood count, serum electrolytes, blood urea nitrogen, creatinine, liver function test.
Transthoracic Echocardiogram showing impaired ventricular function and hemodynamics.

preauth.label: Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment
Blood investigations: Biomarkers, Brain natriuretic peptide (BNP), N-terminal pro-brain natriuretic peptide (NT-proBNP), Creatine kinase

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers with treatment details

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment submitted?
Was the planned line of treatment submitted?
Was the ECG/ECHO with report and Chest X Ray report of patient submitted?
Was the Blood investigations: Biomarkers, Brain natriuretic peptide (BNP), N-terminal pro-brain natriuretic peptide (NT-proBNP), Creatine kinase reports submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Was Detailed Indoor Case Papers with vital (BP and Pulse) and Treatment details submitted?
Was the Detailed Discharge Summary submitted with the date of the follow-up mentioned?

common_queries: Patient history and examination notes not suggestive of congestive heart failure.
Clinical notes, evaluation findings, indications, or planned line of treatment missing or incomplete.
ECG or ECHO report missing or incomplete.
Chest X-ray report missing or incomplete.
Required blood investigation reports (Biomarkers, BNP, NT-proBNP, Creatine kinase) missing or incomplete.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers with vital parameters or treatment details missing for claim submission.
Detailed Discharge Summary missing or follow-up date not mentioned.

clinical_key_pointers: Heart failure is a common and complex clinical syndrome that results from any functional or structural heart disorder, impairing ventricular filling or ejection of blood to the systemic circulation to meet the body's needs.
Heart failure can be caused by diseases of the endocardium, myocardium, pericardium, heart valves, vessels or metabolic disorders.
Most patients with Heart failure have symptoms due to impaired left ventricular myocardial function.
additional_information.referral_criteria: Referral of cases to the appropriate level of care (as required) for treatment of patients under PMJAY.
